title: TwinHarness state.json
description: The mechanical run state owned by the th CLI. This published schema mirrors the hand-rolled validator in src/core/state-schema.ts (kept in sync by tests/schemas.test.ts). The CLI itself does not depend on a JSON-schema engine (zero runtime dependencies).

schema_version.description: State-schema version. Absent on legacy files (implicitly v1); stamped by th init / th migrate.

tier.description: Project tier, or null until classified.

depends_on.description: Slice IDs that must reach done before this slice can build. Absent = no explicit dependencies.

depends_on_soft.description: INTERFACE-only (soft) dependency slice IDs. May be dispatched speculatively against the upstream contract before the upstream is done; the merge-conflict-as-BLOCKING-drift backstop catches bad speculations (REQ-PCO-070). Absent = no soft dependencies.

write_gate.description: PreToolUse write-gate mode. Absent ⇒ ask semantics. strict = deny semantics plus Phase-B Bash-write enforcement (a superset of deny).

project_mode.description: greenfield (default/absent) or brownfield (adopting an existing codebase). Set by th init --brownfield.

interview_threshold.description: Resolved ambiguity-gate threshold for th:run --interview (spec R15). Absent ⇒ default 0.20. Not gate-owned. Omitted from serialization when absent so existing state files hash identically.

delivery_mode.description: Delivery mode (audit finding #2). Absent ⇒ code: a code project must produce implementation slices, so empty slices during the implementation stage are invalid. no-code/documentation-only keep empty slices vacuously settled.

has_ui.description: Whether the project has a UI surface (audit finding #13). Absent ⇒ true (UX/UI stages engaged). When false the ux-design/ui-design stages are not applicable (mechanically satisfied as N/A — no UI surface).

interview_required.description: Whether a clarity interview must reach readiness before advancing past requirements (audit finding #14, soft gate). Absent ⇒ computed from tier (true for T2/T3, false otherwise).
